task:
Fix ticket 0049 â€” stop Deb watch prompts from firing on healthy run boundaries. Owner file: packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts (with status.ts/tests). Re-read these before changing: `wakeDeb` (~L1124), `refreshStatus` (~L1140, currently ALWAYS calls `wakeDeb('status', ...)` at its tail ~L1168), the Deb watcher `onAssessment` (~L1308) and the await-Oscar watchdog `onAssessment` (~L1397-1401 which calls refreshStatus every monitor cadence), and `triageFault` (~L1205, which already sends Deb a separate triage dispatch).

WHAT TO CHANGE:
1. Decouple artifact refresh from Deb pane prompts. `refreshStatus()` must continue to render+write `deb-status.json` AND `deb-terminal-snapshot.json` and record the `deb-status` event on EVERY call (normal boundaries and live waits), but must NO LONGER unconditionally push a `DEB WATCH` pane prompt. Remove the unconditional `wakeDeb('status', ...)` tail.
2. Add an actionable-only wake path. Deb gets a `DEB WATCH` pane prompt ONLY for actionable conditions: an explicit stall / aged-wait assessment (the monitor `onAssessment` non-progressing branch â€” state `stuck`/`blocked`, which only triggers past the nudge grace window) and projection contradictions. Faults are already handled by the existing triage dispatch in `triageFault` â€” do NOT add a second prompt there; `refreshStatus('faulted', ...)` must not also wake Deb. Net: a healthy directive/build/verify/wrap/awaiting-founder boundary refresh sends ZERO pane prompts; an actionable stall sends exactly one.
3. Fix the projection-order bug so `watch.lastDispatch` cannot lag the prompt. Today `refreshStatus` renders+writes `deb-status.json` BEFORE `wakeDeb` records the `deb-watch-dispatch` event, so a fresh `DEB WATCH` prompt names a status file still reporting the PRIOR dispatch. In the new actionable-wake path, record the `deb-watch-dispatch` event BEFORE rendering deb-status.json (or re-render after recording) so that when the prompt tells Deb to read deb-status.json, `watch.lastDispatch` already reflects THIS dispatch. Recommended shape: give `refreshStatus` an optional `wake?: {kind; detail}` arg â€” when present (actionable), record the dispatch event first, then render+write status (lastDispatch current), then sendInput; when absent (normal boundary), just render+write+record deb-status with no prompt. Preserve the existing dedup (`lastDebWakeKey`) and `lastDebWakeAt`/grace bookkeeping in the wake path.
4. Keep the terminal snapshot written on every refresh so it stays available as read-only evidence even when no prompt is sent.

TESTS (pin BOTH sides â€” required by the ticket's Acceptance):
- In packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts: assert healthy directive/build/verify/wrap boundary refreshes write the status + snapshot artifacts and record `deb-status` events but send NO Deb pane `DEB WATCH` input and record NO `deb-watch-dispatch` event.
- Assert an actionable fault still reaches Deb (triage dispatch) and an actionable/aged stall assessment sends exactly ONE Deb `DEB WATCH` prompt (not one per refresh).
- Assert ordering coherence: when the actionable wake prompt is sent, the just-written `deb-status.json` `watch.lastDispatch` equals that dispatch's detail (cannot lag). A status.test.ts case may pin the projection if cleaner.

MUST NOT BREAK: faults still triage exactly once; the Deb watcher still delivers Deb-authored nudges; status artifacts still refresh during live waits; no change to non-Deb runs (debRef null still early-returns). Match surrounding style; keep it minimal and elegant â€” do not invent a new event type if the existing `deb-watch-dispatch` suffices.

ACCEPTANCE / EXIT: `pnpm --dir packages/core test` green for runner.test.ts and status.test.ts (and the rest of the core suite), and `pnpm --dir packages/core typecheck` (or the repo's typecheck) clean. Report the exact commands, exit codes, and the new/changed test names.
